message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@ravikiran.s-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
Team, am checking the issue.
Thanks for jumping on this—let us know what you find as soon as possible.
[p0] Orders per minute is going down. Due to 504 increase in errors
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Use metrics as evidence
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
@bez We have P0 issue in production , seeing a dip in the orders per minute. We are currently debugging will update here once we have fixed it
Need specifics: which regions are impacted and how many customers/orders are affected? Are we currently losing revenue? Please clarify.
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
@shay were there any deployments done in the payment service ?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?
@shay  what was the latest change deployed in the checkout service, which would cause 504 ?

The latest change in checkout service (CHG89360, v1.5.5) was deployed 15 minutes ago and added a new call to the payment service for order validation, but it should not cause 504s unless the payment service is unresponsive.
:bulb: Understand the problem by reproducing it

Unable to do the checkout currently says payment is unavailable. 
Tanaya can you check the issue on the payment service
:star: Know your team and their roles
I'm seeing that the payment service health check is returning 504 errors and latency has spiked, even though CPU and memory usage are normal.
Could it be an issue with payment service providers, if yes do we have an option to change the service provider ?
:star: Form a working theory
The 504 errors indicate our payment service is not responding in time, which could be due to issues with the upstream provider or our integration point.
If the provider is down, we should check if our fallback logic is working as expected or if recent changes affected provider selection.
@daniel see a change deployed for payment service to improve the reliabiltiy , could this be causing the fallback issue ?
:star: Reviewed changes
My change (CHG89362) was deployed 5 minutes ago, but the new code for Bitcoin payments isn't being called yet and all fallback logic is covered by existing tests.
Tanaya how can I verify if fallback logic is working fine ?
:star: Reproduces the incident
You can check the payment service logs for fallback provider invocation and monitor if any alternative payment providers are being called during failures.
can you share the logs reference
:star: Ask team members to provide evidence of their assertions
The payment service logs show repeated 504 errors with no evidence of fallback provider calls being triggered after the recent deployment.
Recent deployment related to the checkout Service ?
Yes, the latest checkout service deployment (CHG89360, v1.5.5) went live 15 minutes ago and introduced a new call to the payment service for order validation.
@shay lets revert the deployment then
I will rollback my checkout change right away
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
I see a spike in the frontend error on the grafana
Customer complaints are still coming in from the UK, and our dashboard shows no improvement after the rollback.
The spike in frontend errors aligns with the ongoing payment failures, so we should look deeper into recent backend changes affecting UK payment flows.
@bez issue is observed in UK
Understood, UK is affected. Quantify the impact—how many customers or orders are down? Are we losing revenue at this moment? Need numbers.
@daniel though the changes were deployed 5 mins ago, can we revert and see if this will solve the issue for UK payments
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
Yes
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
Tanaya also there was a change in the prod database, can you give more details on the same
I'm not aware of any recent production database changes related to payments, but I can check with the DB team if you want more details.
@dain

Drill Wrap-Up

@daniel post your revert things are back to normal.
The issue is resolved now .

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
